verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is synthesized as a secreted protein (signal peptide, no TM domain) but is produced predominantly in a latent complex with its pro-peptide and latency-associated peptide (LAP), anchored to the cell surface and extracellular matrix via covalent or stable non-covalent interactions with integrins and other TM partners (including LTBP family ECM anchors). The latent complex can be activated (mechanically, proteolytically, or by integrins) to release active TGF-β, and there is documented evidence of TGFB1 surface display in the context of cell-surface-associated latent complexes. Additionally, activated immune cells and tumor cells display surface-associated TGFB1. The protein body is not intrinsically membrane-anchored but achieves stable, wash-resistant surface residency via covalent / non-covalent association with integrin TM partners and ECM-resident LTBP scaffolds.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Ambiguity between `stable_surface_attachment` (latent TGFB1 tethered to integrins / LTBP anchors on cell surface) and `secreted_only` (if the ECM-resident form and equilibrium with the soluble pool dominate the biological story over cell-surface-anchored complexes). Most TGFB1 exists as a secreted latent complex deposited in ECM; the cell-surface integrin-bound pool is real but quantitatively minor. The wash-resistance and covalent/stable non-covalent tethering to TM integrin partners place it in `stable_surface_attachment` rather than transient recruitment.
